questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: At $25^\circ$C a solution has $\text{pOH}=4.2$. Its pH is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{pH}+\text{pOH}=14$ at $25^\circ$C.

**Working.**
$$\text{pH}=14-4.2=9.8.$$

**Answer.** $9.8$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.1}=6.93.$$

**Answer.** $6.93\,\text{d}$

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.231}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A water contains $40\,\text{mg/L}$ of $\text{Ca}^{2+}$ and $12\,\text{mg/L}$ of $\text{Mg}^{2+}$. The total hardness as $\text{CaCO}_3$ is _____ mg/L. (Eq. wt: Ca 20, Mg 12.15, CaCO₃ 50)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to $\text{CaCO}_3$: $\text{mg/L}\times\dfrac{50}{\text{eq.wt}}$.

**Working.**
$$40\times\dfrac{50}{20}+12\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=149.4.$$

**Answer.** $149.4\,\text{mg/L as CaCO}_3$

questions.stem: For a dilute aqueous solution (density $\approx 1\,\text{g/mL}$), a concentration of $1\,\text{mg/L}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In dilute water $1\,\text{mg/L}=1\,\text{mg/kg}=1\,\text{ppm}$.

**Working.**
1 mg/L = 1 ppm.

**Answer.** 1 ppm

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Which species are associated with photochemical (Los-Angeles type) smog?
options: Sulphur dioxide as the chief agent
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Photochemical smog is an oxidising NOₓ/VOC–sunlight system producing O₃ and PAN; SO₂ characterises reducing/London smog.

**Working.**
O₃, PAN, NOₓ.

**Answer.** Ozone, PAN and NOₓ

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Between pH ~6.3 and 10.3 the carbonate system is dominated by $\text{HCO}_3^-$.

**Working.**
Bicarbonate dominates.

**Answer.** Bicarbonate

$$N_a=\dfrac{0.2\times25}{25}=0.2.$$

**Answer.** $0.2\,\text{N}$
